summary_oneline: Odoo-aware code extractor, SQLite persistence backend, and graph reader for navigable Odoo repositories

localization.role: Add mtime param, sha1 stamping, lineno/end_lineno to domain_tags
Wire extractor selection, mtime, SQLite backend

constraints.title: Backward compatibility of CodeExtractor changes
constraints.description: mtime is Optional kwarg, sha1/lineno are additive domain_tags keys. All existing callers remain unaffected.

constraints.title: OdooCodeExtractor must fall back to base for non-Odoo classes
constraints.description: The brainstorm correctly calls super()._extract_class() when no Odoo signals detected. This is critical for mixed repos.

constraints.title: Canonical model nodes use synthetic source_uri
constraints.description: odoo-model:// URIs prevent replace_document_slice from orphaning cross-module edges. This invariant is well-reasoned.

constraints.title: ArangoDB parity for EXTENDS edges
constraints.description: meta_ontology needs gi_extends collection + RelationDef, but only if ArangoDB must also persist EXTENDS. SQLite backend handles it natively.

constraints.title: Explicit dependency declarations
constraints.description: aiosqlite and orjson should be added to the graphindex extra in pyproject.toml for clean dependency boundaries.

new.role: Per-tenant SQLite persistence backend with WAL, FTS5, is_stale()
HOT rustworkx topology + COLD FTS5/BM25 search + disk-based source resolution

changes.change: Add extends -> gi_extends to EDGE_KIND_TO_COLLECTION + RelationDef
Add mtime kwarg, sha1 stamping, lineno/end_lineno in domain_tags
Configurable extractor, mtime passing, SQLite backend selection
Add aiosqlite and orjson to graphindex extra

untouched: Embedding pipeline (embed.py) — no embeddings in v1
XML/OWL/JS parsing — possible v2 via tree-sitter multi-language

confidence_map.claim: EdgeKind.EXTENDS is a one-line addition to schema.py
RelationType is trivially extensible (FEAT-239 just added 4 members)
SQLitePersistence does NOT exist yet — must be implemented
Builder persistence is injected, so SQLite backend swaps in cleanly
All runtime dependencies (rustworkx, aiosqlite, orjson, tree-sitter) are available
Brainstorm's OdooCodeExtractor design is sound and follows CodeExtractor patterns
Brainstorm's SQLiteGraphReader design (HOT/COLD split) is sound
19 existing tests provide a strong test-pattern baseline for new tests
Extractor selection in builder is hardcoded — needs refactoring for Odoo support

unknowns.question: Should OdooCodeExtractor be selectable per-build or should it always replace CodeExtractor (with fallback)?
plausible_answers: Always use OdooCodeExtractor — it falls back to base for non-Odoo classes
Make extractor configurable via builder constructor param
Auto-detect based on presence of __manifest__.py files

unknowns.question: Should aiosqlite be added as an explicit dependency in the graphindex extra, or rely on the asyncdb transitive?
plausible_answers: Add explicitly to graphindex extra for clean boundaries
Rely on asyncdb transitive — fewer deps to manage

recommended_next_command.rationale: The brainstorm is detailed and well-grounded. All localization is high-confidence. The main correction (SQLitePersistence scope) is clear. A spec can be written directly from this proposal.
